description: High school biology course focusing ecology, cell theory, cellular respiration, cell energy, cell growth, immunity and genetics.

topics.name: Unit 1: Introduction to Life Science
topics.description: Foundational concepts of life science, including the characteristics of living things, scientific methods, themes of biology, and the organization of life from atoms to the biosphere.

topics.name: Unit 2: Ecology & Ecosystems
topics.description: Interactions among organisms and their environment, including energy flow, biogeochemical cycles, population dynamics, community ecology, and ecosystem stability.

topics.name: Unit 3: Chemistry of Life
topics.description: Basic chemical principles for biology, properties of water, pH, organic molecules, and the structure and function of carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids.

topics.name: Unit 4: Cell Structure & Function
topics.description: Cell theory, prokaryotic vs eukaryotic cells, organelle structure and function, plant vs animal cells, microscopy, and cellular specialization.

topics.name: Unit 5: Cell Membrane & Transport
topics.description: Structure of the cell membrane, passive transport (diffusion, osmosis, facilitated diffusion), active transport (pumps, endocytosis, exocytosis), and tonicity.

topics.name: Unit 6: Cell Energy
topics.description: Energy transformations in cells, ATP structure and function, photosynthesis (light reactions, Calvin cycle), and cellular respiration (glycolysis, Krebs cycle, electron transport chain).

topics.name: Unit 7: Cell Growth & Division
topics.description: Cell cycle, mitosis, cytokinesis, regulation of the cell cycle, cancer, and an introduction to meiosis and its role in sexual reproduction.

topics.name: Unit 8: Infectious Disease & Immunity
topics.description: Pathogens (bacteria, viruses, etc.), modes of transmission, immune system structures and responses, vaccines, antibiotics, and public health.

topics.name: Unit 9: Genetics & Heredity
topics.description: Mendelian genetics, Punnett squares, non-Mendelian inheritance, chromosomes, DNA structure, replication, gene expression, and genetic technology.

topics.name: Unit 10: Evolution & Biodiversity
topics.description: Evidence for evolution, natural selection, mechanisms of evolution, speciation, classification systems, and patterns of biodiversity.
